Latest News Latest News Archives Monthly newsletter About Stephen About Stephen About Stephen Stephen's Expenses The Rt Hon Sir Stephen Timms is the Member of Parliament for East Ham, and the Minister of State for Social Security and Disability. He is also the Labour Party’s Faith Envoy. He has held various ministerial posts under previous Labour Governments, including Chief Secretary to the Treasury, Financial Secretary to the Treasury, and Minister of State for Schools. Stephen entered Parliament in 1994 and has proudly represented the people of East Ham ever since. Before entering Parliament, Stephen worked in the telecommunications industry for fifteen years, first for Logica and then for Ovum. Stephen also worked as a councillor in Newham for ten years, serving as Newham Council leader from 1990 until his election in 1994. Stephen received a Knighthood in the late Queen’s Birthday Honours in 2022 for his contributions to politics and public service “ It is good to know we have an MP who is always ready to act on our behalf when we need it. ” — Mr and Mrs Ismail, Local residents T: 020 7219 4000 | E: Email stephen here Back to Top Promoted by the Labour Party on behalf of David Evans, General Secretary of the Labour Party, 105 Victoria Street, Westminster, London SW1E 6QT

Latest News Latest News Archives Monthly newsletter Need Help? If you require my assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me via one of the two routes below. I will do my best to assist in any way I can. Before doing so, please click here to check that I am your local Member of Parliament. I receive a lot of requests for assistance and so, to be fair to all, I take each piece of casework in the order that it was received. I aim to take action within ten working days but often do so much quicker. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to give my office a call on 0207 219 4000. Advice surgeries I hold regular advice surgeries in East Ham. You can book an appointment by contacting my office on 0207 219 4000 or completing the adjacent form. Online You can also request assistance by submitting the online form below. All fields marked * are mandatory. Click here to read my acceptable behaviour policy. Click here to read my website privacy policy. T: 020 7219 4000 | E: Email stephen here Back to Top Promoted by the Labour Party on behalf of David Evans, General Secretary of the Labour Party, 105 Victoria Street, Westminster, London SW1E 6QT

Latest News Latest News Archives Monthly newsletter Latest News Stephen Timms March 30, 2026 Stephen: New health centre funding "will make a difference" Stephen Timms March 30, 2026 Hush Naidoo Jade Photography via Unsplash | @hush52 Stephen has welcomed plans for a new neighbourhood health centre serving residents in Manor Park, Little Ilford, East Ham and nearby communities. The new Centre Manor Park will bring a wide range of services together in one place, making it easier for local people to access care close to home. The centre will host GP services, pharmacy provision and urgent treatment facilities under one roof. Over time, it is expected to offer additional support such as employment advice, debt guidance and family services. Bringing these services together aims to address some of the pressures that contribute to poor health locally while helping to reduce demand on hospital departments. The centre will open 12 hours a day, six days a week. It is one of the first group of neighbourhood health centres to be established, with further sites planned across England in the coming years. Speaking about the announcement, Stephen said: “I am very pleased that residents in Manor Park and nearby areas will soon have a new neighbourhood health centre within easy reach. Local people often tell me how difficult it can be to navigate different services when they need support. Bringing GP care, pharmacy services, urgent treatment and wider community advice together in one accessible place will make a real difference for many households. “This centre will be particularly valuable for residents managing long-term or complex conditions, who will benefit from more coordinated support close to home.” Neighbourhood health centres are designed to offer faster and more joined‑up care, helping people receive the support they need earlier and preventing unnecessary hospital visits. Stephen Timms March 30, 2026 Stephen welcomes new "Best Start" nursery in Forest Gate Stephen Timms March 30, 2026 Markus Spiske via Unsplash | @markusspiske Stephen has welcome the creation of a new “Best Start” nursery which will open at Monega Primary School, in Forest Gate. The nursery will create additional childcare places for families in Manor Park and Forest Gate. The expansion is part of a national programme that is supporting more than 300 schools to develop or extend nursery provision from September. School-based nurseries are intended to offer additional childcare close to home, reduce travel time for families, and support parents who are balancing work and caring responsibilities. They also provide a single drop-off location for families with children of different ages. Across the country, 331 schools have been allocated a share of £45 million in capital funding to build or expand nurseries on their sites. The new Best Start nurseries will create more than 6,000 additional childcare places, building on the first phase of the programme. Recent national figures show that over a million parents now use government‑funded childcare schemes. These include funded hours for eligible working parents and free breakfast clubs, which aim to support family budgets and improve access to early education. Speaking about the announcement, Stephen said: “Families in East Ham regularly raise the importance of accessible childcare and support for parents balancing work and family life. I contacted local schools last year to highlight the Best Start programme and the opportunity to expand nursery places in our area. “I am pleased that Monega has now been successful in its application and will be able to provide new childcare places for local families.” Stephen Timms March 30, 2026 Stephen introduces new work pr
